content: When you're hungry, the hypothalamus releases neuropeptide Y and agouti, which trigger electrical impulses in the brain to make you feel hungry.
Neuropeptide Y and agouti are released by the hypothalamus and travel to the brain to trigger electrical impulses that make you feel hungry.
Leptin is a hormone produced by fat cells and it triggers your body to stop being hungry. The more fat you have, the less hungry you are.
Cholecystokinin (CCK) is a hormone produced in the upper portion of the small intestine and it tells your brain to stop eating. It works as a counter to the hunger hormone Grayling.
Insulin is a hormone produced by the pancreas and it obviously is going to cause you to have these blood sugar rises and falls that can trigger other actions on the hypothalamus.
Dopamine is a neurotransmitter that allows us to feel good. When dopamine receptors are deactivated, it eliminates the ability to balance out and you're being one-sided.
